key_insight: Two low-cost surrogate approaches can estimate electron-phonon coupling and thermoelectric transport in semiconductors using far fewer first-principles matrix elements than full calculations.
engineering_relevance: These methods can speed up predictive screening of semiconductor thermoelectrics while preserving accuracy enough for practical materials design.
method: Generalized acoustic deformation-potential model built from ~10 first-principles matrix elements per band/phonon mode.
Machine-learning interpolation of ~100 electron-phonon matrix elements onto dense reciprocal-space grids.
Applied to 2D MoS2 for transport-relevant regions of the Brillouin zone.
evidence_signal: Both approaches agree well with state-of-the-art calculations, and the resulting thermoelectric properties also match experiments closely.
watch_item: The model-based approach requires careful parameterization for arbitrary symmetry and band-extremum locations, while the ML method depends on having enough representative matrix elements near transport-relevant k-points.
paper_title: Generalized deformation potential and machine-learning approaches for electron-phonon coupling and thermoelectric transport in semiconductors
